title: Election materials transported by helicopter
description: MORE HUMLA, MARCH 1 The staff deployed at four polling stations in Humla and the election materials including ballot papers will be transported by helicopter.

title: Bajura's Bichchhya voters warn candidates: no roads, no tower, no vote
description: MORE BAJURA, MARCH 1 Residents of the remote Bichchhya area in Himali Rural Municipality are heading into the upcoming House of Representatives election with a clear message for candidates: no roads, no tower, no vote.

title: Balendra (Balen) Shah’s wave, rather the Rastriya Swatantra Party, has grown in favour in Janakpur, the capital of Madhes province. Santosh Raut, an e-rickshaw driver, stated that people are not having other ideas than ‘Ghanti’, which means ‘bell’, the election symbol of RSP. “However, there is likely to be competition between the Nepali Congress and RSP,” he said. “In my opinion, Ghanti is winning.”
description: MORE JANAKPUR, FEBRUARY 27 Balendra (Balen) Shah's wave, rather the Rastriya Swatantra Party, has grown in favour in Janakpur, the capital of Madhes province. Santosh Raut, an e-rickshaw driver, stated that people are not having other ideas than 'Ghanti', which means 'bell', the election symbol of RSP. "However, there is likely to be competition between the Nepali Congress and RSP," he said. "In my opinion, Ghanti is winning."

title: Home Minister Om Prakash Aryal has reiterated the government’s commitment to conducting the HoR election in a free, fair, and peaceful environment.
description: MORE KATHMANDU, FEBRUARY 27 Home Minister Om Prakash Aryal has reiterated the government's commitment to conducting the House of Representatives (HoR) election in a free, fair, and peaceful environment.

title: The District Administration Office Kathmandu has issued a renewed notice directing individuals to deposit their licensed firearms at the nearest police office in view of the upcoming House of Representatives election.
description: MORE KATHMANDU, FEBRUARY 27 The District Administration Office Kathmandu has issued a renewed notice directing individuals to deposit their licensed firearms at the nearest police office in view of the upcoming House of Representatives election.
